channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel tells NBC News that he will not step down
item.description: Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has told NBC News' Meet the Press that he would not step down in his first interview with a U.S. network.
item.content:encoded:
Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel has told NBC News' Meet the Press that he would not step down in his first interview with a U.S. network.

(Image credit: Adalberto Roque)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: A fragile ceasefire and emboldened Iran
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with International correspondent Daniel Estrin about how the US-Iran-Israel ceasefire is being received in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Mary Louise Kelly

item.title: How Trump's foreign policy is playing in North Carolina
item.description: What do people who follow foreign affairs make of the war with Iran and the president's foreign policy? NPR spoke with a dozen World Affairs Council members in North Carolina to find out.
item.content:encoded: What do people who follow foreign affairs make of the war with Iran and the president's foreign policy? NPR spoke with a dozen World Affairs Council members in North Carolina to find out.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: How did Pakistan bring Iran and the U.S. to the negotiating table?
item.description: Pakistan has acted as a mediator between Washington D.C. and Tehran over the last few weeks, and helped broker the ceasefire agreement this week. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Elizabeth Threlkeld, a senior fellow and director at the Stimson Center, about why Pakistan decided to step into this role.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan has acted as a mediator between Washington D.C. and Tehran over the last few weeks, and helped broker the ceasefire agreement this week. NPR's Juana Summers talks to Elizabeth Threlkeld, a senior fellow and director at the Stimson Center, about why Pakistan decided to step into this role.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Pierre

item.title: In the Trump era, everybody's talking about 'soft power.' But ... what is it exactly?
item.description: The U.S. government long saw giving international aid as a way to build goodwill throughout the world. Did it work? And what does the reducing of foreign aid mean for that effort now?
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. government long saw giving international aid as a way to build goodwill throughout the world. Did it work? And what does the reducing of foreign aid mean for that effort now?

(Image credit: Ben de la Cruz/NPR)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Iran-U.S. ceasefire off to a shaky start. And, Bill Gates to testify in Epstein probe
item.description: Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks persist across the Middle East despite the two-week ceasefire deal between the U.S. and Iran. And, Bill Gates is set to testify in the investigation of the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief about the future of the partnership
item.description: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday to discuss the future partnership after Trump disparaged the alliance.
item.content:encoded: President Trump met with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte Wednesday to discuss the future partnership after Trump disparaged the alliance.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Israel vows to continue fighting Hezbollah but agrees to talks with Lebanon
item.description: Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.

(Image credit: Fadel Itani)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.
item.content:encoded: Trump and NATO chief meet to discuss the partnership, attacks continue across the Gulf region despite U.S.-Iran ceasefire, Iran says ceasefire includes Lebanon, but Israel and Trump say it's not.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Iran expert discusses whether the war has made the Iranian regime stronger
item.description: Iran expert Vali Nasr of Johns Hopkins University discusses whether the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has made the Iranian regime stronger.
item.content:encoded:
Iran expert Vali Nasr of Johns Hopkins University discusses whether the U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has made the Iranian regime stronger.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
